title: Weird behaviour of podman auto-update
body:
Hi guys,

I'm not sure if this is a bug, but I have completely no clue, what happened here, so I would really appreciate I someone could take a look at it.

I'm running latest `Debian 12` and `podman 4.3.1`. I used `podman generate systemd --new --files --name` to generate systemd services, because quadlet is not yet available in Debian.

Here are the systemd units where the issue appeared:

```
# pod-01_reverse-proxy.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.3.1
# Tue Oct  8 20:31:00 CEST 2024

[Unit]
Description=Podman pod-01_reverse-proxy.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/run/user/1000/containers
Wants=container-01_reverse-proxy_caddy.service
Before=container-01_reverse-proxy_caddy.service

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm \
	-f %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pid %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pod-id
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/podman pod create \
	--infra-conmon-pidfile %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pid \
	--pod-id-file %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pod-id \
	--exit-policy=stop \
	--replace \
	--no-hosts \
	--network host 01_reverse-proxy
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman pod start \
	--pod-id-file %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pod-id
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman pod stop \
	--ignore \
	--pod-id-file %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pod-id  \
	-t 10
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman pod rm \
	--ignore \
	-f \
	--pod-id-file %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pod-id
PIDFile=%t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

```
# container-01_reverse-proxy_caddy.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.3.1
# Tue Oct  8 20:31:00 CEST 2024

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-01_reverse-proxy_caddy.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers
BindsTo=pod-01_reverse-proxy.service
After=pod-01_reverse-proxy.service

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm \
	-f %t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman container run \
	--cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id \
	--cgroups=no-conmon \
	--rm \
	--pod-id-file %t/pod-01_reverse-proxy.pod-id \
	--sdnotify=conmon \
	-d \
	--replace \
	--name 01_reverse-proxy_caddy \
	--label io.containers.autoupdate=registry \
	-v /home/admin/apps/01_reverse-proxy/caddy/Caddyfiles/:/etc/caddy/ \
	-v /home/admin/apps/01_reverse-proxy/caddy/config/:/config/ \
	-v /home/admin/apps/01_reverse-proxy/caddy/data/:/data/ docker.io/library/caddy:latest
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop \
	--ignore -t 10 \
	--cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm \
	-f \
	--ignore -t 10 \
	--cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Here is what happend. On 2 AM `podman auto-update` automatically runs (systemd timer). Restarting the container after updating did not work because of a podman error I don't know where it came from:

```

The rollback did also not change anything. After a couple tries it stopped trying to restart the service.

This is not what I'm wondering about. Maybe the container did not stopped correctly, that can happen I guess. At 5:22 AM I did a reboot. After that, it started correctly. But it was still running the old version, so I did another `podman auto-update` manually:

```

That failed without any error from podman or the application (caddy). Very weird. I tried a couple times, to understand whats going on.

After a 4 tries that gave the exact ouput as above, It just worked. I changed nothing, I just run `podman auto-update` and it worked:

comments.body:
We only support the latest version upstream, I guess you are hitting this:
https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/6dd797844249b7ffa1b422fd0cd49f562e017772
